message:
[medium] dropbox: reject base_url and content_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

DropboxConfig::from_params stored params["base_url"] and
params["content_url"] verbatim with no URL-shape validation.
base_url_policy was only consulted in provisioning_readiness
(advisory — it does not gate handle_configure). A configure payload
like

    {"access_token": "t", "base_url": "https://api.dropboxapi.com/2?leak=x"}

would pass from_params and flow into DropboxClient::new where base_url
is concatenated via format!("{}{endpoint}", self.base_url)
(client.rs:191). `?leak=x` would leak on every Dropbox API request
with the endpoint path parsed as part of the query. Userinfo
(`https://attacker:pw@api.dropboxapi.com/2`) would bake into every
request URL and silently override the OAuth Authorization header.
content_url is a separate override with the same downstream shape,
so both surfaces are hardened symmetrically.

- New reject_base_url_qfu(field, url) helper parameterized on the
  field name, called from DropboxConfig::from_params for both base_url
  and content_url. Rejects userinfo (username or password) and
  query/fragment components at configure time. Host-allowlist
  advisory in base_url_policy is preserved unchanged.
- Six new unit tests: accept clean base+content URLs, reject base_url
  query / fragment / userinfo, reject content_url query / userinfo.

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo check -p
fcp-dropbox --tests is still blocked by the upstream asupersync main
E0252 regression at src/runtime/waker.rs:128. Dropbox diff reviewed
manually: additions use std url::Url accessor calls with FcpError
shapes identical to the surrounding validator branches.
reject_base_url_qfu follows the same pattern as the bitbucket /
intercom helpers this session, with the field-name parameter added
so error messages distinguish base_url from content_url.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
